name.description: Name of the SparkApplication and the status provisioner Pod

create.description: Whether to create a separate ServiceAccount for the SparkApplication and the status provisioner Pod. If false, the default ServiceAccount in the namespace will be used.

name.description: Name of the ServiceAccount to use for the SparkApplication and the status provisioner Pod. This is only used if serviceAccount.create is true. If serviceAccount.create is false, this value is ignored and the default ServiceAccount will be used.

repository.description: Container image repository for the SparkApplication. The image tag will be determined by the 'tag' field. The image should contain the necessary test scripts and dependencies to run the Spark integration tests.

tag.description: Container image tag for the SparkApplication. The image should contain the necessary test scripts and dependencies to run the Spark integration tests.

sparkAppsNamespace.description: Kubernetes namespace where the SparkApplication will be created. This namespace should exist before deploying the chart.

status_writing_enabled.description: Whether to enable the status provisioner for writing SparkApplication status updates to a custom resource. If true, a status provisioner Pod will be created that will monitor the SparkApplication and update its status in a custom resource. If false, no status provisioner will be created and the SparkApplication status will not be written to a custom resource.

is_short_status_message.description: Whether to write a short status message to the custom resource.

only_integration_tests.description: Whether to write status updates to the custom resource only during the execution of integration tests. If true, the status provisioner will only write status updates to the custom resource while the integration tests are running. Once the tests complete, the provisioner will stop writing updates and can optionally be deleted based on the 'lifetimeAfterCompletion' setting. If false, the provisioner will continue to write status updates to the custom resource even after the integration tests have completed, until it is manually deleted or reaches its lifetime limit.

status_custom_resource_path.description: The API path of the custom resource where the status provisioner will write SparkApplication status updates. This should be a valid Kubernetes API path for a custom resource that can store the status information. The provisioner will use this path to update the status of the SparkApplication in the specified custom resource.

prometheusUrl.description: URL of the Prometheus server to scrape metrics from the SparkApplication. If not provided, metrics will not be scraped or exposed.

resources.description: Compute Resources required by spark-integration-tests container. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

sparkAppImage.description: Container image for the SparkApplication. The image should contain the necessary test scripts and dependencies to run the Spark integration tests.

sparkPyAppImage.description: Container image for the PySparkApplication. The image should contain the necessary test scripts and dependencies to run the PySpark integration tests.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context for the spark-integration-tests Pod.

priorityClassName.description: Priority class name for the spark-integration-tests Pod. If null, the Pod will not have a priority class and will be scheduled with default priority. If set to a valid priority class name, the Pod will be scheduled with the priority defined by that class.

securityContext.description: Container-level security context for the status provisionor Pod.

testsS3Endpoint.description: Endpoint URL for the S3-compatible storage used in integration tests. This should be the URL of the MinIO server or any other S3-compatible storage service that will be used to store test data during the Spark integration tests.

testsS3AccessKey.description: Access key for the S3-compatible storage used in integration tests. This should be the access key for the MinIO server or any other S3-compatible storage service that will be used to store test data during the Spark integration tests.

testsS3SecretKey.description: Secret key for the S3-compatible storage used in integration tests. This should be the secret key for the MinIO server or any other S3-compatible storage service that will be used to store test data during the Spark integration tests.

endpointUrl.description: Endpoint URL for the S3-compatible storage used in integration tests. This should be the URL of the MinIO server or any other S3-compatible storage service that will be used to store test data during the Spark integration tests.

accessKey.description: Access key for the S3-compatible storage used in integration tests. This should be the access key for the MinIO server or any other S3-compatible storage service that will be used to store test data during the Spark integration tests.

secretKey.description: Secret key for the S3-compatible storage used in integration tests. This should be the secret key for the MinIO server or any other S3-compatible storage service that will be used to store test data during the Spark integration tests.

bucketName.description: Name of the S3 bucket used in integration tests.

dbName.description: Name of the database used in integration tests.

tableName.description: Name of the table used in integration tests.

enabled.description: Whether to enable Volcano integration tests. If true, the Spark integration tests will include tests that verify the integration between the Spark Operator and Volcano scheduler. If false, these tests will be skipped.

Whether to enable Spark-Hive integration tests. If true, the Spark integration tests will include tests that verify the integration between the Spark Operator and Hive. If false, these tests will be skipped.

sparkHiveImage.description: Docker image for the Spark-Hive integration tests.
